response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a long career, but his expertise is overwhelmingly in TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies — not in Java, Kotlin, or the JVM ecosystem this role demands. His brief Java exposure at Altom Consulting was limited to robotic arm control software, and his only IDE development experience was writing C/C++ components for a C IDE at National Instruments, which is architecturally unrelated to JVM-based IDE tooling. There is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience anywhere in his history.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline: TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing across multiple roles
Some adjacent IDE and developer tooling experience: NI LabWindows C IDE work and MCP code generator/framework contributions
Long and diverse engineering career showing adaptability, ownership, and the ability to work on complex, multi-language systems
concerns: Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js — Java and Kotlin are peripheral mentions with no substantial production JVM work
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework required by the role
No IntelliJ Platform, Eclipse, or JVM-based IDE internals experience; the only IDE work was in C for a non-JVM tool

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
